tasks.title: Create project scaffold and config files
tasks.description:
Create all project configuration files from scratch — no source files exist yet.

1. `package.json`:
   - `"name": "claude-usage-tracker"`, `"displayName": "Claude Usage Tracker"`, `"description": "Track your Claude Code token usage and cost"`, `"version": "0.0.1"`
   - `"engines": { "vscode": "^1.85.0" }`, `"main": "./dist/extension.js"`, `"activationEvents": ["*"]`
   - `"contributes": { "commands": [{ "command": "claudeUsage.openDashboard", "title": "Claude Usage: Open Dashboard" }] }`
   - `"scripts": { "build": "node esbuild.mjs", "watch": "node esbuild.mjs --watch", "package": "vsce package" }`
   - `"devDependencies": { "esbuild": "latest", "typescript": "latest", "@types/vscode": "^1.85.0", "@types/react": "^18", "@types/react-dom": "^18" }`
   - `"dependencies": { "react": "^18", "react-dom": "^18" }`

2. `tsconfig.json`:
   ```json
   { "compilerOptions": { "target": "ES2020", "module": "commonjs", "jsx": "react", "strict": true, "esModuleInterop": true, "outDir": "dist" }, "include": ["src", "webview"] }
   ```

3. `.gitignore`: include `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `*.vsix`

4. `.vscodeignore`: include `src/**`, `webview/**`, `node_modules/**`, `.ralph/**`, `.claude/**`, `esbuild.mjs`, `tsconfig.json`

tasks.title: Create esbuild build system (esbuild.mjs)
tasks.description:
Create `esbuild.mjs` at the project root that performs two independent builds:

1. Extension bundle: entry `src/extension.ts` → output `dist/extension.js`
   - format: `cjs`, platform: `node`, external: `['vscode']`, bundle: true, sourcemap: true

2. Webview bundle: entry `webview/App.tsx` → output `dist/webview.js`
   - format: `iife`, platform: `browser`, bundle: true, sourcemap: true

Support `--watch` mode: if `process.argv.includes('--watch')`, call `context.watch()` on both esbuild contexts instead of a one-shot `build()`. Use the esbuild context API (`esbuild.context(...)`) for both cases so watch mode works correctly.

This file is required before any TypeScript compilation or testing.

tasks.title: Create shared TypeScript types (src/types.ts)
tasks.description:
Create `src/types.ts` exporting the `UsageEntry` interface used by all extension-side modules:

```typescript
export interface UsageEntry {
  timestamp: string;             // ISO string from entry.timestamp
  sessionId: string;             // entry.sessionId (UUID)
  cwd: string;                   // entry.cwd (full path, e.g. "/Users/ivan/my-project")
  model: string;                 // entry.message.model
  inputTokens: number;           // entry.message.usage.input_tokens
  outputTokens: number;          // entry.message.usage.output_tokens
  cacheCreationTokens: number;   // entry.message.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens ?? 0
  cacheReadTokens: number;       // entry.message.usage.cache_read_input_tokens ?? 0
}
```

This file is imported by `usageParser.ts`, `costCalculator.ts`, `statusBar.ts`, and `dashboardPanel.ts`. Keep it to just this interface — no logic.

tasks.title: Implement JSONL data parser (src/usageParser.ts)
tasks.description:
Create `src/usageParser.ts` that reads all Claude Code session files and returns an array of `UsageEntry`.

Imports: `UsageEntry` from `./types`, `fs`, `path`, `os` from Node.

Logic for `export function parseUsageData(): UsageEntry[]`:
1. Resolve base dir: `path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude', 'projects')`
2. Recursively find all `*.jsonl` files using a helper `walkDir(dir: string): string[]` that uses `fs.readdirSync` + `fs.statSync` to recurse
3. For each file, read with `fs.readFileSync(file, 'utf8')`, split on `'\n'`, skip empty lines
4. For each line, wrap `JSON.parse` in try/catch; skip malformed lines silently
5. Accept only entries where `entry.type === 'assistant'` and `entry.message?.usage` is truthy
6. Map to `UsageEntry`:
   - `timestamp`: `entry.timestamp`
   - `sessionId`: `entry.sessionId`
   - `cwd`: `entry.cwd`
   - `model`: `entry.message.model ?? 'unknown'`
   - `inputTokens`: `entry.message.usage.input_tokens ?? 0`
   - `outputTokens`: `entry.message.usage.output_tokens ?? 0`
   - `cacheCreationTokens`: `entry.message.usage.cache_creation_input_tokens ?? 0`
   - `cacheReadTokens`: `entry.message.usage.cache_read_input_tokens ?? 0`
7. Return the flat array of all collected entries

Note: If `~/.claude/projects/` does not exist, return `[]` without throwing.

tasks.title: Implement cost calculator (src/costCalculator.ts)
Create webview HTML template (webview/index.html)
tasks.description:
Create `webview/index.html` — the HTML scaffold loaded by `dashboardPanel.ts`. The file uses a placeholder `{{WEBVIEW_JS_URI}}` that `dashboardPanel.ts` replaces at runtime with a VS Code webview-safe URI via `panel.webview.asWebviewUri`. ```html Claude Usage Dashboard
``` The inline style uses VS Code CSS variables so the dashboard matches the user's theme automatically.

tasks.title: Implement React webview app (webview/App.tsx)
tasks.description:
Create `webview/App.tsx` — the React app bundled into `dist/webview.js` and rendered inside the VS Code webview panel. This file runs in a browser sandbox and must NOT import from `src/`.

Local interface (declare inline, not imported):
```typescript
interface UsageEntry { timestamp: string; sessionId: string; cwd: string; model: string; inputTokens: number; outputTokens: number; cacheCreationTokens: number; cacheReadTokens: number; }
```

Cost calculation: inline pricing logic mirroring `costCalculator.ts` (haiku/opus/default tiers).

App structure:
1. State: `entries: UsageEntry[]` (starts empty), `activeTab: 'today'|'week'|'month'|'all'`
2. `useEffect` on mount: `window.addEventListener('message', handler)` where handler sets entries when `event.data.type === 'update'`
3. Tab filtering:
   - Today: entries where `new Date(e.timestamp).toDateString() === new Date().toDateString()`
   - This Week: entries from last 7 days
   - This Month: entries from last 30 days
   - All Time: all entries
4. Summary cards: 4 cards — Total Input Tokens, Total Output Tokens, Total Cache Tokens (cacheCreationTokens + cacheReadTokens), Estimated Cost — all using `formatTokens` and `formatCost` helpers
5. Bar chart (HTML/CSS only, no library):
   - Today tab: 24 bars (hours 0–23 of today); each bar's height proportional to tokens in that hour
   - Other tabs: daily bars; for week/month/all show days sorted ascending
   - Bar height uses `calc(Xpx)` or percentage based on max value in the set
   - Label each bar with its formatted token count
6. Model breakdown table: columns Model | Sessions (unique sessionId count) | Input Tokens | Output Tokens | Est. Cost
7. Project breakdown table: columns Project (`path.basename(cwd)` logic inline) | Sessions | Tokens | Est. Cost

Mount: `import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(<App />);`

tasks.title: Implement dashboard panel (src/dashboardPanel.ts)
Implement status bar item (src/statusBar.ts)
Implement extension entry point (src/extension.ts)
